events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "tub drain line / vanity trap",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Unvented S-trap configuration created by immediate vertical drop after the trap weir",
    "Lack of a horizontal trap arm of minimum required length (2 times the pipe diameter)",
    "Siphoning of the trap seal indicated by gurgling noises when other fixtures drain"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the vertical drop immediately following the P-trap",
    "Install a sanitary tee in the wall or cabinet back to establish a proper horizontal trap arm",
    "Install an Air Admittance Valve (AAV) or tie a new vent line into the existing DWV system"
  ],
  "rationale": "The installation of an S-trap is prohibited by modern plumbing codes (IPC and UPC) because the vertical drop immediately following the trap weir creates a siphon that pulls water out of the trap, breaking the liquid seal and allowing sewer gases to enter the living space. The gurgling sound indicates that the trap is losing its seal when pressure fluctuations occur in the drainage system.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 1002.3 (Prohibited Traps)",
    "IPC Section 909.2 (Vent Distance from Trap Weir)",
    "UPC Section 1004.1 (Prohibited Traps)"
  ]
}
